questions.question: In triangle ABC, angle A measures 58° and angle B measures 67°. What is the measure of angle C in degrees?
questions.explanation: The sum of angles in any triangle is 180°. So angle C = 180° − 58° − 67° = 55°.

questions.question: A right triangle has one acute angle measuring 34°. What is the measure of the other acute angle in degrees?
questions.explanation: In a right triangle, the two acute angles are complementary (sum to 90°). So the other angle = 90° − 34° = 56°.

questions.question: In an isosceles triangle, the vertex angle (the angle between the two equal sides) measures 64°. What is the measure of each base angle in degrees?
questions.explanation: The two base angles of an isosceles triangle are equal. If the vertex angle is 64°, then 2b + 64 = 180, so b = 58°.

questions.question: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. If one of the acute angles formed measures 73°, what is the measure of an obtuse angle formed at a different intersection point?
questions.explanation: When a transversal crosses parallel lines, an acute angle and an obtuse angle are supplementary. The obtuse angle = 180° − 73° = 107°.

questions.question: An exterior angle of a triangle measures 128°. One of the non-adjacent interior angles measures 51°. What is the measure of the other non-adjacent interior angle in degrees?
questions.explanation: An exterior angle equals the sum of the two non-adjacent interior angles. So 128° = 51° + x, giving x = 77°.

questions.question: In a 30-60-90 triangle, the side opposite the 30° angle has length 5. What is the length of the hypotenuse?
questions.explanation: In a 30-60-90 triangle, the sides are in ratio 1 : √3 : 2. The side opposite 30° is the shortest, so if it is 5, the hypotenuse is 2 × 5 = 10.
